analysisSummary: Confirmed. The original XDG path selection trusted directory existence but not writability, which breaks runtime state creation under read-only runtime dirs and can drop guard enforcement into degraded behavior.
suggestedFix: Probe candidate XDG directories with a real file create/remove step before accepting them, and fall back to the next writable location when the probe fails.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Session artifact parsing reused default bufio.Scanner behavior across multiple JSONL readers, so valid large artifact lines broke context-resume and sharp-edge query flows in the same way the transcript parser did.
suggestedFix: Use a shared high-capacity scanner helper for all session JSONL readers and keep regression tests on the affected resume/query paths.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Routing transcript parsing treated each JSONL line as a scanner token without increasing the default cap, so valid high-volume tool events broke both routing/task-analysis code paths and metadata extraction for workflow endstate handling.
suggestedFix: Use a shared scanner helper with an explicit multi-megabyte token limit anywhere transcript JSONL is consumed line-by-line.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Team-run execution returned success for final-wave partial failures because only all-member failure was treated as fatal. That leaks failed member state past the command boundary and misreports the overall run outcome.
suggestedFix: Keep intermediate partial failures non-fatal so later synthesizer waves can run, but return an error if failed members remain after the final wave completes.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. The bridge's socket-path builder did not account for Unix pathname socket length limits, so certain valid runtime/temp directory configurations prevented the TUI bridge from binding at startup.
suggestedFix: Select the first runtime/temp candidate whose full socket path fits within the Unix socket path limit, and fall back to a short path such as /tmp when needed.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Telemetry analytics reused default bufio.Scanner behavior across multiple JSONL readers, so valid large telemetry records were dropped or surfaced as read failures across invocations, escalations, scout, collaboration, and lifecycle loaders.
suggestedFix: Use a shared enlarged scanner helper for telemetry JSONL readers and keep direct large-line regressions on the public loader functions.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Workflow endstate log reading relied on the default scanner cap even though serialized recommendation payloads can exceed it, breaking valid analytics input.
suggestedFix: Use a dedicated enlarged scanner for endstate JSONL reads and keep the large-entry regression in logging_test.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Failure-tracker scanning powered both counting and clearing operations and previously used the default scanner limit, making large valid entries effectively invisible to loop detection.
suggestedFix: Switch failure-tracker reads to a shared enlarged scanner and keep a regression with a >64 KiB error_match field.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Persistent data directory selection still trusted directory existence instead of actual writability, so telemetry and ML logging could fail at first write even after path selection succeeded.
suggestedFix: Probe candidate data directories with a real create/remove write test before returning them, and fall back to the next writable location.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Permission-gate cache filenames incorporated raw session IDs from stdin events, so slash-containing IDs altered the resolved cache path and escaped the intended runtime directory.
suggestedFix: Derive cache filenames from a path-safe stable encoding such as a cryptographic hash of session_id rather than embedding session_id directly.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. Weekly aggregation counted archived JSONL entries with the default scanner cap, so a single large valid line degraded artifact counts in the generated summary.
suggestedFix: Use an enlarged scanner buffer in countLines and keep a regression on a >64 KiB archived line.

analysisSummary: Confirmed. The TUI routing telemetry panel still used the default scanner cap after backend readers were fixed, so valid large decisions could vanish from the UI or surface as load errors.
suggestedFix: Use an enlarged scanner buffer in the panel's JSONL loader and keep the large-entry regression in telemetry_test.
